mnemonics: B V Ramana's Groups Are Finite: B V Ramana stands for Binary operation, Verification of axioms (Closure, Associativity, Identity, Inverse), and Finiteness (order of group and elements).
keyPoints: A group is a set with a binary operation that is closed, associative, has an identity element, and every element has an inverse.
The order of a group is the number of elements in it. The order of an element is the smallest positive integer n such that a^n = e (the identity).
Groups can be abelian (commutative) or non-abelian. All cyclic groups are abelian.
commonMistakes: Assuming all groups are abelian (commutative). Many important groups, like permutation groups, are non-abelian.
Forgetting to check the closure property when determining if a set with an operation is a group.
Confusing the order of a group (number of elements) with the order of an element (smallest n such that a^n = e).
